heading: Payment of tax by commercially acceptable means
label: 6311 Payment of tax by commercially acceptable means
notes: Amendments2019—Subsec. (d)(2). inserted at end “The preceding sentence shall not apply to the extent that the Secretary ensures that any such fee or other consideration is fully recouped by the Secretary in the form of fees paid to the Secretary by persons paying taxes imposed under subtitle A with credit, debit, or charge cards pursuant to such contract. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, the Secretary shall seek to minimize the amount of any fee or other consideration that the Secretary pays under any such contract.”Pub. L. 116–252018—Subsec. children.heading: Authority to receive
children.label: a Authority to receive
children.text: It shall be lawful for the Secretary to receive for internal revenue taxes (or in payment for internal revenue stamps) any commercially acceptable means that the Secretary deems appropriate to the extent and under the conditions provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Ultimate liability
children.label: b Ultimate liability
children.text: If a check, money order, or other method of payment, including payment by credit card, debit card, or charge card so received is not duly paid, or is paid and subsequently charged back to the Secretary, the person by whom such check, or money order, or other method of payment has been tendered shall remain liable for the payment of the tax or for the stamps, and for all legal penalties and additions, to the same extent as if such check, money order, or other method of payment had not been tendered.

children.heading: Liability of banks and others
children.label: c Liability of banks and others

children.text: the amount of such check (or draft) upon all assets of the financial institution on which drawn,
the amount of such money order upon all the assets of the issuer thereof, or
the guaranteed amount of any other transaction upon all the assets of the institution making such guarantee,

children.label: A
children.text: specify which methods of payment by commercially acceptable means will be acceptable,

children.label: B
children.text: specify when payment by such means will be considered received,

children.label: C
children.text: identify types of nontax matters related to payment by such means that are to be resolved by persons ultimately liable for payment and financial intermediaries, without the involvement of the Secretary, and

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that tax matters will be resolved by the Secretary, without the involvement of financial intermediaries.

children.heading: Authority to enter into contracts
children.label: 2 Authority to enter into contracts
children.text: Notwithstanding , United States Code, the Secretary is authorized to enter into contracts to obtain services related to receiving payment by other means where cost beneficial to the Government. The Secretary may not pay any fee or provide any other consideration under any such contract for the use of credit, debit, or charge cards for the payment of taxes imposed by subtitle A. The preceding sentence shall not apply to the extent that the Secretary ensures that any such fee or other consideration is fully recouped by the Secretary in the form of fees paid to the Secretary by persons paying taxes imposed under subtitle A with credit, debit, or charge cards pursuant to such contract. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, the Secretary shall seek to minimize the amount of any fee or other consideration that the Secretary pays under any such contract.section 3718(f) of title 31

children.heading: Special provisions for use of credit cards
children.label: 3 Special provisions for use of credit cards
A
children.text: a payment of internal revenue taxes (or a payment for internal revenue stamps) by a person by use of a credit card shall not be subject to section 161 of the Truth in Lending Act (), or to any similar provisions of State law, if the error alleged by the person is an error relating to the underlying tax liability, rather than an error relating to the credit card account such as a computational error or numerical transposition in the credit card transaction or an issue as to whether the person authorized payment by use of the credit card,15 U.S.C. 1666

children.label: B
children.text: a payment of internal revenue taxes (or a payment for internal revenue stamps) shall not be subject to section 170 of the Truth in Lending Act (), or to any similar provisions of State law,15 U.S.C. 1666i

children.label: C
children.text: a payment of internal revenue taxes (or a payment for internal revenue stamps) by a person by use of a debit card shall not be subject to section 908 of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (), or to any similar provisions of State law, if the error alleged by the person is an error relating to the underlying tax liability, rather than an error relating to the debit card account such as a computational error or numerical transposition in the debit card transaction or an issue as to whether the person authorized payment by use of the debit card,15 U.S.C. 1693f

children.label: D
children.text: the term “creditor” under section 103(g) of the Truth in Lending Act () shall not include the Secretary with respect to credit card transactions in payment of internal revenue taxes (or payment for internal revenue stamps), and15 U.S.C. 1602(g)

children.label: E
children.text: notwithstanding any other provision of law to the contrary, in the case of payment made by credit card or debit card transaction of an amount owed to a person as the result of the correction of an error under section 161 of the Truth in Lending Act () or section 908 of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (), the Secretary is authorized to provide such amount to such person as a credit to that person’s credit card or debit card account through the applicable credit card or debit card system.15 U.S.C. 166615 U.S.C. 1693f

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise authorized by this subsection, no person may use or disclose any information relating to credit or debit card transactions obtained pursuant to section 6103(k)(9) other than for purposes directly related to the processing of such transactions, or the billing or collection of amounts charged or debited pursuant thereto.

children.label: A
children.text: Debit or credit card issuers or others acting on behalf of such issuers may also use and disclose such information for purposes directly related to servicing an issuer’s accounts.

children.label: i
children.text: statistical risk and profitability assessment;

children.label: ii
children.text: transferring receivables, accounts, or interest therein;

children.label: iii
children.text: auditing the account information;

children.label: iv
children.text: complying with Federal, State, or local law; and

children.label: v
children.text: properly authorized civil, criminal, or regulatory investigation by Federal, State, or local authorities.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 3 Procedures
children.text: Use and disclosure of information under this paragraph shall be made only to the extent authorized by written procedures promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 4 Cross reference
children.text: For provision providing for civil damages for violation of paragraph (1), see section 7431.
